expectations.text: Defines a simulator that generates AR(2) time series of length 100
expectations.evidence: Lines 34, 51-62: T=100, observation_model generates AR(2) via y[t] = phi1*y[t-1] + phi2*y[t-2] + noise. Line 65: simulator = bf.make_simulator([prior, observation_model]).

expectations.text: Routes the time series through summary_variables (not inference_conditions)
expectations.evidence: Line 113: .concatenate(["y"], into="summary_variables"). No routing of y into inference_conditions anywhere.

expectations.text: Does NOT use SetTransformer for the time series data
expectations.evidence: Line 84: Uses bf.networks.TimeSeriesTransformer. No occurrence of SetTransformer in the file.

expectations.text: Uses TimeSeriesTransformer, TimeSeriesNetwork, or FusionTransformer as the summary network
Calls .constrain('sigma', lower=0) for the noise standard deviation
Does NOT flatten the time series into a single vector for inference_conditions
expectations.evidence: Line 108: .as_time_series(["y"]) reshapes to (T,1). Line 113: concatenated into summary_variables. No flattening into inference_conditions.

expectations.text: Starts with the Small model configuration from references/model-sizes.md
expectations.evidence: Lines 84-92: embed_dims=(32,32), num_heads=(2,2), mlp_depths=(1,1), mlp_widths=(64,64), time_embed_dim=4. Line 96: subnet widths=(128,128). Comments on lines 82-83 reference 'Small config from model-sizes.md'.

expectations.text: Passes validation_data= to the fit_* call
Saves and inspects training history (JSON + inspect_training or equivalent)
expectations.evidence: Lines 141-143: json.dump(history.history, f) saves to history.json. Lines 145-147: inspect_history(history.history) called and report printed.

expectations.text: Runs in-silico diagnostics and checks against house thresholds
expectations.evidence: Lines 157-171: workflow.simulate(300) generates test data, workflow.compute_default_diagnostics() computes metrics, check_diagnostics(metrics) checks thresholds, RuntimeError raised on STOP decision.

expectations.text: Accesses posterior samples by original names (phi1, phi2, sigma), NOT 'inference_variables'
expectations.evidence: Lines 197-200: samples["phi1"], samples["phi2"], samples["sigma"]. Lines 209-211: same pattern. No samples["inference_variables"] anywhere in the file.
